summary: Iteration 53: Bredent SKY Drilling Protocol Testing - 21/21 tests PASSED (100%). All 5 Bredent SKY systems (Mini 2 Sky, Copa Sky, Narrow Sky, Blue Sky, Sky Classic) verified working correctly with bone-adaptive logic.

minor.issue: miniSKY implant placement note doesn't include '25-45 Ncm' torque value (other 3 systems do include it)

frontend_issues.note: Backend-only testing per request. Frontend has local protocol generation in CaseImplantPlanning.tsx for Bredent SKY systems.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add '25-45 Ncm' torque to miniSKY implant placement note for consistency with other Bredent systems
critical_code_review_comments: Feature 1 (GET /api/drilling-protocols/available): PASS - All 5 Bredent systems listed: Mini 2 Sky, Copa Sky, Narrow Sky, Blue Sky, Sky Classic
Feature 2 (miniSKY D1): PASS - Pilot → Twist 2.25 → Final → Implant (NO crestal for any bone type)
Feature 3 (miniSKY D4): PASS - Final drill at 50 RPM anticlockwise for condensation
Feature 4 (copaSKY): PASS - Simplified ultra-short protocol: Pilot → Final → Implant only
Feature 5 (copaSKY depth): PASS - Depth = 5.9mm (5.2 + 0.7)
Feature 6 (narrowSKY D1): PASS - NO crestal drill for D1 hard bone
Feature 7 (narrowSKY D2-D4): PASS - WITH crestal drill (FULL insertion)
Feature 8 (blueSKY D1): PASS - NO crestal drill for D1 hard bone
Feature 9 (blueSKY D2-D4): PASS - WITH crestal drill
Feature 10 (blueSKY diameters): PASS - Works for 4.0, 4.5, 5.5mm
Feature 11 (classicSKY D1): PASS - NO crestal drill for D1 hard bone
Feature 12 (classicSKY D2-D4): PASS - WITH crestal drill
Feature 13 (classicSKY diameters): PASS - Works for 4.0, 4.5mm
Feature 14 (Depth Rule): PASS - All systems use depth = implant_length + 0.7mm
Feature 15 (Implant Placement Depth): PASS - Implant depth = implant_length (no offset)
Feature 16 (D4 Condensation): PASS - Final drill 50 RPM anticlockwise for Mini 2 Sky, Narrow Sky, Blue Sky, Sky Classic
Feature 17 (Insertion Torque): PASS - 25-45 Ncm in notes for narrowSKY, blueSKY, classicSKY
Feature 18 (PDF Export): PASS - All 5 systems return valid PDF
Feature 19 (Protocol Type Labels): PASS - D1=Hard Bone, D2/D3=Standard, D4=Condensation
Feature 20 (Regression Cowellmedi INNO): PASS - Still works correctly
Feature 21 (Regression MIS Lance +): PASS - Still works correctly
seed_data_creation: No test data created - read-only tests on drilling protocol generation
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bredent SKY drilling protocols fully implemented and tested. Key implementation details: DRILLING_PROTOCOLS entries at lines 4625-4658 in server.py, _generate_bredent_protocol function at line 4661. Protocol rules: (1) Depth = Implant Length + 0.7mm, (2) Implant placement depth = implant_length, (3) D1 = NO crestal drill, D2-D4 = WITH crestal drill (FULL insertion), (4) D4 = Final drill 50 RPM anticlockwise for condensation, (5) Self-cutting, no tap required, (6) Insertion torque 25-45 Ncm. System-specific: miniSKY uses Twist 2.25mm and has NO crestal for any bone type; copaSKY is simplified ultra-short (Pilot → Final → Implant only); narrowSKY/blueSKY/classicSKY share common pattern with bone-adaptive crestal drill logic. DB system names: 'Mini 2 Sky', 'Copa Sky', 'Narrow Sky', 'Blue Sky', 'Sky Classic'. Brand is 'Bredent'. MIS system name is 'Lance +' (with space before +).

tested_features.available_protocols: PASS - All 5 Bredent systems in available protocols
tested_features.minisky_protocol: PASS - Pilot → Twist 2.25 → Final → Implant (no crestal for any bone)
tested_features.copasky_protocol: PASS - Simplified ultra-short: Pilot → Final → Implant
tested_features.d4_condensation: PASS - 50 RPM anticlockwise for all systems except copaSKY
tested_features.pdf_export: PASS - All 5 systems return valid PDF

tested_endpoints.GET /api/drilling-protocols/available: 200 - Returns all 5 Bredent systems with correct metadata

narrow_sky.sequence: Pilot → Twist 2.8 → Final → [Crestal if D2-D4] → Implant

blue_sky.sequence: Pilot → Twist 2.8 → Final → [Crestal if D2-D4] → Implant

sky_classic.sequence: Pilot → Twist 2.8 → Final → [Crestal if D2-D4] → Implant
